name: Salesforce
description: The dominant enterprise CRM; sales, service, and platform APIs on one metadata-driven backend.
selfServeDetail: Free Developer Edition org self-signup; Connected Apps issue OAuth2 client credentials instantly.
mcp_notes: Salesforce ships an official 'Agentforce'/MCP-adjacent toolset; several community MCP servers wrap the REST/Bulk API.
notes: One of the deepest API surfaces in the set (REST, Bulk, Streaming, GraphQL via Connect API, Metadata API).

name: HubSpot
description: CRM plus marketing/sales/service hubs, built around a public app marketplace.
selfServeDetail: Free HubSpot account + free developer test account; Private App tokens issued instantly, no approval.
mcp_notes: HubSpot publishes an official MCP server (developers.hubspot.com).

name: Pipedrive
description: Pipeline-first sales CRM aimed at SMB sales teams.
selfServeDetail: Free 14-day trial account; API token available from Settings > Personal Preferences immediately.
mcp_notes: Community MCP servers exist; no official Pipedrive MCP found.

name: Attio
description: Modern, flexible/relational CRM popular with startups.
selfServeDetail: Free workspace signup; API keys generated in-app immediately, no approval step.
mcp_notes: Attio ships an official MCP server for its API.

name: Twenty
description: Open-source, self-hostable CRM positioned as an Airtable-like alternative to Salesforce.
selfServeDetail: Fully open-source; self-host or use hosted cloud, generate an API key from workspace settings with zero gating.
mcp_notes: Community MCP wrappers exist for Twenty's GraphQL API; no first-party MCP confirmed at time of research.
notes: Open source nature makes this one of the most agent-friendly, low-friction CRMs in the set.

name: Podio
description: Citrix-owned flexible work-management/CRM platform built on custom 'apps' (structured data objects).
selfServeDetail: Free account + self-service API key request form (near-instant approval, not a sales gate).
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: Product is in long-term maintenance mode; docs are dated and API key requests are manual (short delay, not instant).

name: Zoho CRM
description: Full CRM inside the broader Zoho One suite, popular with SMBs.
selfServeDetail: Free Zoho account + Zoho API Console self-registration for OAuth client credentials.
mcp_notes: No official Zoho MCP found; community wrappers exist for Zoho APIs generally.

name: Close
description: Inside-sales CRM with built-in calling/email, API-first by design.
auth: API Key (Basic auth w/ key as username), OAuth2
selfServeDetail: Free trial signup; API key generated instantly from Settings > API Keys.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found at time of research.

name: Copper
description: CRM built natively into Google Workspace for relationship-driven sales teams.
selfServeDetail: Free trial account; API key generated in-app under Settings > API Keys, no approval.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: DealCloud
description: Deal/relationship-management CRM for investment banks, PE, and dealmakers (part of Intapp).
selfServeDetail: Enterprise-only platform sold directly by Intapp; API access is provisioned per-client during implementation, no public self-signup.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found; docs live behind a client login wall.
blocker: Enterprise-only, contact-sales gate; API docs require an existing client login, so schema/auth cannot be independently confirmed pre-contract.
notes: Low confidence: could not access docs content behind login; classification based on DealCloud's public go-to-market model.

name: Zendesk
description: Category-defining ticketing/helpdesk platform with a huge public API surface.
selfServeDetail: Free trial account; API token generated instantly in Admin Center.
mcp_notes: Zendesk has previewed an official MCP server for its API.

name: Intercom
description: Customer messaging platform combining support inbox, chat, and AI agent (Fin).
selfServeDetail: Free trial account; personal access token generated instantly under Settings > Developer.
mcp_notes: Intercom publishes an official MCP server.

name: Freshdesk
description: Freshworks helpdesk/ticketing product, SMB-focused alternative to Zendesk.
selfServeDetail: Free trial account; API key visible instantly under profile settings.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community connectors exist.

name: Front
description: Shared inbox / collaborative email platform for support and account teams.
selfServeDetail: Trial account; API token generated in Settings > Developers, self-serve.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Pylon
description: Modern B2B support platform unifying Slack, Teams, and Discord-based customer support.
selfServeDetail: Free/paid workspace signup required, but API tokens can only be created by workspace Admin users -- an internal approval step, not a sales gate.
mcp_notes: Pylon documents an official 'Pylon MCP' entry in its developer nav.
notes: Live-checked during verification sample: confirmed Bearer auth, admin-only token creation, per-endpoint rate limits, and an official MCP entry in the docs nav.

name: LiveAgent
description: Multi-channel helpdesk (email, chat, call center, social) aimed at SMBs.
selfServeDetail: Free trial; API key generated instantly in Configuration > System > API.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Plain
description: Developer-first, API-native support tool built around Slack-based support workflows.
selfServeDetail: Self-serve account signup; API key generated instantly in workspace settings. Product is explicitly API/GraphQL-first.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
notes: GraphQL-only API -- one of the few in the set with no REST surface at all.

name: Help Scout
description: Lightweight shared-inbox helpdesk favored by small support teams.
selfServeDetail: Free trial account; OAuth2 app created instantly in My Apps developer console.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Gorgias
description: Helpdesk built specifically for ecommerce (deep Shopify/BigCommerce integration).
selfServeDetail: Free trial account; Basic-auth API keys self-generated, OAuth2 required only for public marketplace apps.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Gladly
description: Enterprise, customer-centric (not ticket-centric) support platform.
selfServeDetail: No free tier; API access ships with paid contracts, and credentials are issued during onboarding by Gladly, not via public self-signup.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: No free/trial tier for independent API testing; requires an active paid contract to obtain credentials.

name: Slack
description: The default team-chat platform and one of the richest bot/app platforms in the industry.
selfServeDetail: Free workspace + free developer app creation at api.slack.com/apps, zero approval for personal/internal use.
mcp_notes: Slack has an official MCP server in preview alongside many community ones.

name: Twilio
description: Programmable SMS/voice/video communications infrastructure, the archetype API-first company.
auth: API Key + Secret, Basic Auth (Account SID + Auth Token)
selfServeDetail: Free trial account with test credentials issued instantly on signup; full self-serve console.
mcp_notes: Twilio publishes an official Alpha MCP server.

name: Zoho Cliq
description: Team chat product inside the Zoho suite, with a bots/webhooks platform.
selfServeDetail: Free Zoho account; OAuth client created self-serve in Zoho API Console; incoming webhooks configurable without any approval.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Lark (Larksuite)
description: ByteDance's all-in-one workspace suite (chat, docs, calendar) with a dedicated Open Platform.
auth: OAuth2, App Access Token / Tenant Access Token
selfServeDetail: Free developer account on the Open Platform; app credentials self-issued instantly, no partner approval for internal/self-built apps.
mcp_notes: No confirmed official MCP; community MCP servers wrap Lark's Open Platform APIs.

name: Pumble
description: Free-forever team chat app positioned as a Slack alternative for SMBs.
selfServeDetail: Pumble documents incoming webhooks for posting messages, but no general-purpose public REST API for reading/managing workspace data was found.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: No documented general REST/GraphQL API for agent-style read/write access -- only one-directional incoming webhooks were confirmed.

name: Discord
description: Community/gaming-native chat platform with a very large bot ecosystem.
selfServeDetail: Free Developer Portal signup; bot tokens issued instantly, no approval for standard bot scopes.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; multiple community MCP servers exist.

name: Telegram
description: Messaging app whose Bot API is one of the most agent-friendly integrations available.
selfServeDetail: Bot token issued instantly via @BotFather in the app itself -- no developer account or approval of any kind.
mcp_notes: Many community MCP servers wrap the Bot API.

name: WhatsApp Business
description: Meta's business messaging API for WhatsApp, delivered via Cloud API or BSP partners.
selfServeDetail: Free Meta developer account and Cloud API test number are self-serve, but production sending requires Meta Business verification and phone number registration -- an approval gate, not a partnership.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community servers exist.
blocker: Production access requires Meta Business Verification, a manual review that can take days and reject applicants.

name: Aircall
description: Cloud call-center/VoIP platform for sales and support teams.
selfServeDetail: Free trial account; Basic auth API credentials self-generated in Integrations > API Keys.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Vonage
description: Communications APIs (SMS, voice, video) similar in spirit to Twilio.
selfServeDetail: Free developer account with trial credit; keys issued instantly on signup.
mcp_notes: No official MCP confirmed; community servers exist.

name: Google Ads
description: Google's advertising platform API for managing campaigns programmatically.
selfServeDetail: Free Google Cloud + Ads account, but a Developer Token requires Google's manual approval (basic access is fast; 'Standard' access for production scale needs a review).
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community MCP servers wrap the Google Ads API.
blocker: Developer Token approval step and strict use-case review for production-level access.

name: Meta Ads
description: Meta's Marketing API for managing Facebook/Instagram ad campaigns.
selfServeDetail: Free developer account and sandbox access are self-serve, but standard access to marketing permissions requires Meta App Review.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community wrappers exist.
blocker: Meta App Review is required for the advertising permissions and can take 1-2+ weeks.

name: LinkedIn Ads
description: LinkedIn's Marketing Developer Platform for managing campaigns and audiences.
selfServeDetail: Requires applying to and being approved for the LinkedIn Marketing Developer Program before any API access is granted -- a formal partner application, not just app review.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: Marketing Developer Program partner approval is required before any credentials are issued; historically slow and selective.

name: GoHighLevel
description: All-in-one sales/marketing platform (CRM, funnels, automations) for agencies.
selfServeDetail: Free trial sub-account; OAuth2 app self-registered in the Marketplace developer console for private/internal use, no approval required unless publishing publicly.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community servers exist given HighLevel's popularity with agencies.

name: Mailchimp
description: Email marketing/automation platform, one of the most widely integrated marketing tools.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier account; API key self-generated instantly in Account > Extras > API keys.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community servers wrap Marketing API.

name: Klaviyo
description: Ecommerce-focused email/SMS marketing automation platform.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier account; private API key generated instantly in Settings > API Keys.
mcp_notes: Klaviyo has published an official MCP server.

name: systeme.io
description: All-in-one funnel builder / course platform / email marketing tool for solo creators.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier account; API key self-generated in Settings > Public API.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Pinterest
description: Visual discovery/social platform with an API for content and advertising.
selfServeDetail: Free developer account; standard-access API app self-registered with no manual approval, though some scopes need app review.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Threads (Meta)
description: Meta's text-based social app; the Threads API allows posting and basic insights.
selfServeDetail: Free developer account and basic self-testing access are available, but public/production use requires Meta App Review for Threads scopes.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: Meta App Review gate for production Threads permissions.

name: SendGrid
description: Transactional and marketing email delivery API (Twilio-owned).
selfServeDetail: Free-tier account (100 emails/day); API key self-generated instantly.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community servers exist.

name: Shopify
description: The dominant ecommerce platform, with one of the most mature app/API ecosystems in SaaS.
auth: OAuth2, Admin API access token (custom apps)
selfServeDetail: Free Partner account + free dev store; custom app access token generated instantly for private/internal integrations.
mcp_notes: Shopify ships an official Storefront/Admin MCP server.

name: WooCommerce
description: Open-source WordPress ecommerce plugin, self-hosted, with a REST API.
auth: API Key + Secret (OAuth1.0a over HTTP, Basic over HTTPS)
selfServeDetail: Fully self-hosted/open-source; keys generated directly in the WordPress admin with zero external approval.
mcp_notes: Community MCP servers exist wrapping the REST API.

name: BigCommerce
description: Headless-friendly, enterprise-capable ecommerce SaaS platform.
selfServeDetail: Free trial store; API account (client ID/secret/token) self-generated instantly in the store admin.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community wrappers exist.

name: Salesforce Commerce Cloud
description: Enterprise ecommerce platform (formerly Demandware) inside the Salesforce family.
selfServeDetail: No public self-signup sandbox for the full commerce stack; access is provisioned as part of an enterprise Salesforce Commerce contract.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: Enterprise-only provisioning; no free/self-serve sandbox for independent testing.

name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
description: Open-source (Magento Open Source) and enterprise (Adobe Commerce) ecommerce platform.
selfServeDetail: Magento Open Source is free and self-hosted; admin token self-generated with zero external approval. Adobe Commerce (paid tier) is contact-sales.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
notes: Split verdict by tier: Open Source edition is Easy/self-serve; Adobe Commerce enterprise edition is Hard/contact-sales.

name: Squarespace
description: Website/ecommerce builder aimed at creators and small businesses.
selfServeDetail: Free trial site; Commerce API keys self-generated in Settings > Advanced > API Keys.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Ecwid
description: Lightweight, embeddable ecommerce storefront (part of Lightspeed).
selfServeDetail: Free-tier store; token self-issued instantly through the app's OAuth flow in the Ecwid Control Panel.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Gumroad
description: Simple storefront for creators selling digital products.
selfServeDetail: Free account; access token self-generated instantly under Settings > Advanced, no approval.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Amazon Selling Partner API
description: API for Amazon marketplace sellers to manage orders, listings, and fulfillment.
selfServeDetail: Requires an active, approved Amazon Seller/Vendor account plus Developer registration and per-role authorization; not gated behind a partnership, but has real onboarding friction (identity verification, role-based approval for restricted operations).
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community servers exist.
blocker: Requires an active Amazon seller account and role-based data-access approval (e.g. restricted PII operations need extra sign-off).

name: fanbasis
description: Payments/checkout infrastructure ('merchant of record') for creators and digital-product sellers.
selfServeDetail: Self-serve dashboard signup; API key generated instantly under Account > API Keys, full sandbox environment available.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
notes: Live-checked during verification sample: confirmed self-serve API key from dashboard, REST + webhooks, official SDKs, sandbox mode.

name: DataForSEO
description: SEO/SERP/keyword data API aimed squarely at developers and agencies.
selfServeDetail: Free account signup with pay-as-you-go credits; credentials available immediately, no approval.
mcp_notes: DataForSEO publishes an official MCP server.

name: SE Ranking
description: SEO/rank-tracking SaaS with a paid API add-on.
selfServeDetail: API access is an add-on module that must be enabled on a paid plan; key is self-generated once enabled, no manual approval beyond payment.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: API is a paid add-on, not available on the free tier -- no way to obtain a free-tier key for testing.

name: Ahrefs
description: Leading backlink/SEO research tool with a metered API.
selfServeDetail: API access requires an active paid Ahrefs subscription with API credits; no free-tier key issuance.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: No free tier; a paid Ahrefs plan is required before any API key can be issued.

name: MrScraper
description: Managed web-scraping API (proxy + headless rendering) for developers.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier signup with limited credits; API key generated instantly.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
notes: Smaller/newer product; docs are thinner than category leaders, confidence kept moderate.

name: Apify
description: Web-scraping/automation platform built around a marketplace of 'Actors' (serverless scrapers).
selfServeDetail: Free tier with monthly credits; API token generated instantly in Settings > Integrations.
mcp_notes: Apify ships an official MCP server exposing Actors as tools.

name: Firecrawl
description: LLM-ready web scraping/crawling API purpose-built for feeding AI agents and RAG pipelines.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier signup with starter credits; API key generated instantly on signup.
mcp_notes: Firecrawl publishes an official MCP server -- itself part of the standard agentic-research toolchain.

name: Bright Data
description: Enterprise proxy network and web-data collection platform.
auth: API Key (Bearer), Zone credentials (username/password for proxies)
selfServeDetail: Free trial credit on signup; API key and proxy zone credentials self-generated instantly in the control panel.
mcp_notes: Bright Data publishes an official MCP server for its Web Unlocker/SERP APIs.

name: Sherlock
description: Open-source CLI tool that hunts for usernames across social networks (OSINT).
selfServeDetail: Not a hosted SaaS/API at all -- it's an installable Python CLI/library; 'access' just means installing the package.
mcp_notes: Community MCP wrappers exist that expose Sherlock as an agent tool.
blocker: Not a hosted API/toolkit-native integration; would need to be wrapped as a subprocess/CLI-invoking tool rather than called over HTTP, and its scraping approach is fragile against target-site changes.
notes: Structurally different from every other row: a CLI tool, not a SaaS API.

name: Waterfall.io
description: B2B contact/company data enrichment API aggregating 30+ underlying data vendors.
selfServeDetail: Usage API takes a self-serve API key with no long-term contract required, but Waterfall states publicly it is 'very selective' about partnering with platforms that want to build products on top of its data -- so building a general-purpose toolkit integration (vs. a single internal workflow) requires their approval.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: Company explicitly gates platform/reseller-style API use behind a selective partnership review, even though direct usage is API-key self-serve.
notes: Live-checked during verification sample: confirmed API-key auth and no-contract usage, but also found the explicit partnership-gate language for platform builders on their own site FAQ.

name: Clay
description: Data enrichment/GTM automation workspace that orchestrates 100+ underlying data providers.
auth: API Key, Webhooks (incoming, for Clay 'HTTP API' tables)
selfServeDetail: Free-tier workspace signup with limited credits; Clay's own API/webhook tables are usable immediately from the workspace.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; Clay is more often the *consumer* of other tools' MCP servers than a provider.

name: GitHub
description: The default code-hosting/collaboration platform, with a deep REST + GraphQL API.
auth: OAuth2, Personal Access Token, GitHub App (JWT)
selfServeDetail: Free account; PAT generated instantly in Settings > Developer Settings, zero approval.
mcp_notes: GitHub publishes an official, widely-used MCP server.

name: Vercel
description: Frontend cloud/deployment platform for Next.js and other frameworks.
selfServeDetail: Free Hobby account; Access Token self-generated instantly in Account Settings > Tokens.
mcp_notes: Vercel publishes an official MCP server.

name: Netlify
description: Static site hosting/deployment platform, direct Vercel competitor.
selfServeDetail: Free tier; PAT self-generated instantly in User Settings > Applications.
mcp_notes: No official MCP confirmed; community servers exist.

name: Cloudflare
description: CDN/security/edge-compute platform (DNS, Workers, Zero Trust, etc.).
selfServeDetail: Free tier; scoped API Tokens self-generated instantly in the dashboard.
mcp_notes: Cloudflare publishes official MCP servers (Workers, Docs, etc.).

name: Supabase
description: Open-source Firebase alternative: Postgres, auth, storage, and edge functions as a managed platform.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier project self-created instantly; API keys visible immediately in project settings.
mcp_notes: Supabase publishes an official MCP server for its management API.

name: Neo4j
description: Graph database; programmatic access is via the Bolt protocol/drivers rather than a general REST API.
auth: Basic Auth / Bearer (drivers), API Key (Aura management API)
selfServeDetail: Free AuraDB Free tier self-created instantly; connection credentials available immediately. The management/admin API for provisioning instances also self-serve via API key.
apiSurface_breadth: broad (as a database, not a typical SaaS API)
mcp_notes: Neo4j publishes an official MCP server for querying via Cypher.
blocker: Primary access pattern is a database driver protocol (Bolt/Cypher), not a typical REST endpoint, so integration effort differs from most other rows even though it is self-serve.

name: Snowflake
description: Cloud data warehouse with SQL API and increasingly agent-oriented Cortex features.
selfServeDetail: Free 30-day trial account with credits; SQL API access configured self-serve via key-pair auth, no manual approval.
mcp_notes: Snowflake publishes an official Cortex/Snowflake MCP server.

name: MongoDB Atlas
description: Managed MongoDB cloud platform, with both a database driver interface and an Atlas Administration API.
auth: Digest Auth (API keys) for Admin API, SCRAM/X.509 for database driver
selfServeDetail: Free M0 cluster self-created instantly; Atlas Admin API keys self-generated in Organization Access Manager, no approval.
mcp_notes: MongoDB publishes an official MCP server.

name: Datadog
description: Observability platform (metrics, logs, traces, monitors) with a comprehensive API.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier trial; API key and App key self-generated instantly in Organization Settings.
mcp_notes: Datadog publishes an official MCP server.

name: Sentry
description: Error-tracking/application-monitoring platform.
selfServeDetail: Free developer tier; Auth Token self-generated instantly in Settings > Auth Tokens.
mcp_notes: Sentry publishes an official MCP server.

name: Notion
description: Docs/wiki/database workspace with a widely-used public API and integration ecosystem.
selfServeDetail: Free personal workspace; Internal Integration token self-generated instantly at notion.so/my-integrations.
mcp_notes: Notion publishes an official MCP server.

name: Airtable
description: Spreadsheet-database hybrid with a very developer-friendly REST API.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier account; PAT self-generated instantly at airtable.com/create/tokens.
mcp_notes: Airtable publishes an official MCP server.

name: Linear
description: Issue-tracking tool built for fast-moving software teams, GraphQL-first API.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier workspace; Personal API key self-generated instantly in Settings > API.
mcp_notes: Linear publishes an official MCP server.

name: Jira
description: Atlassian's issue-tracker/project-management tool, ubiquitous in enterprise engineering orgs.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier Cloud site; API token self-generated instantly at id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens.
mcp_notes: Atlassian publishes an official Rovo/Jira MCP server.

name: Asana
description: Task/project management tool with a mature public API.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier workspace; PAT self-generated instantly in My Settings > Apps > Manage Developer Apps.
mcp_notes: Asana publishes an official MCP server.

name: Monday.com
description: Work-OS style project/workflow management platform, GraphQL API.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier account; API token self-generated instantly under Admin > API.
mcp_notes: monday.com publishes an official MCP server.

name: ClickUp
description: All-in-one work-management platform with a broad REST API.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier workspace; personal token self-generated instantly in Settings > Apps.
mcp_notes: ClickUp publishes an official MCP server.

name: Coda
description: Doc/spreadsheet/app hybrid workspace with a REST API for reading and writing tables.
selfServeDetail: Free-tier account; API token self-generated instantly in Account Settings.
mcp_notes: No official MCP confirmed; community servers exist.

name: Smartsheet
description: Spreadsheet-based work-management platform popular in enterprise operations teams.
selfServeDetail: Free trial account; API access token self-generated instantly in Account > Personal Settings > API Access.
mcp_notes: No official MCP confirmed; community servers exist.

name: Harvest
description: Time-tracking and invoicing tool for services businesses.
selfServeDetail: Free trial account; PAT self-generated instantly at id.getharvest.com/developers.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.

name: Stripe
description: The reference-standard payments API; broad, deeply documented, and API-first by design.
selfServeDetail: Free account; test-mode API keys visible instantly on signup, live keys unlock after basic account activation.
mcp_notes: Stripe publishes an official MCP server.

name: Plaid
description: Bank-account connectivity/financial-data API used by fintech apps.
selfServeDetail: Free Sandbox + Development environment self-serve on signup; Production access requires an application review (fraud/compliance screening).
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community servers exist.
blocker: Production access requires Plaid's manual application review for compliance reasons, even though Sandbox is instant.

name: Binance
description: Major cryptocurrency exchange with a broad public trading/market-data API.
selfServeDetail: Free account (KYC required for trading, not for market-data-only access); API key self-generated instantly.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community servers exist.

name: Paygent Connect
description: Payment orchestration layer described as NMI-powered; positioned for agencies/SaaS platforms reselling payments.
auth: API Key (inherits NMI Direct Post/Query API model)
selfServeDetail: Could not locate an independent, public developer-docs site distinct from the underlying NMI gateway it is built on; onboarding appears to run through a reseller/ISO signup process rather than public self-serve signup.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: No independently verifiable public API documentation found; onboarding appears to require becoming a reseller/merchant account holder first.
notes: Unknown/low-confidence: this is a defeated-app case. Evidence URL points to the underlying NMI platform docs since a distinct public Paygent Connect API reference could not be confirmed.

name: iPayX
description: FX-forensic-audit layer (FINTRAC-registered) that detects hidden bank/card markup on currency transactions.
auth: None (free comparison endpoint), Bearer Token / API Key (paid audit endpoints)
selfServeDetail: One core comparison tool is callable with no auth at all (free); deeper forensic-audit and full-report endpoints require a Bearer/API key and hit a 402 Payment Required after a free-trial allowance -- self-serve, metered by usage rather than gated by approval.
mcp_notes: iPayX ships an official MCP server ('iPayX FX Audit') listed on public MCP directories (Glama, mcpservers.org, GitHub).
notes: Live-checked during verification sample via its public MCP listing: confirmed a no-auth free tier, Bearer-token paid tier, and an official first-party MCP server -- unusual for how small/new this vendor is.

name: QuickBooks
description: Small-business accounting software with a mature public API.
selfServeDetail: Free developer account and Sandbox company self-serve instantly at developer.intuit.com; production keys require a short automated app review (not a sales gate).
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community servers exist.

name: Xero
description: Cloud accounting software popular outside the US, strong API ecosystem.
selfServeDetail: Free developer account and demo company self-serve instantly at developer.xero.com.
mcp_notes: No official MCP; community servers exist.

name: Brex
description: Corporate card/spend-management platform for startups and enterprises.
selfServeDetail: API access requires an active Brex account (business underwriting/approval to become a customer at all); once a customer, API keys are self-generated in the dashboard by an admin.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: Must be an underwritten Brex customer before API keys can be created at all -- not a pure developer-sandbox self-serve.

name: Ramp
description: Corporate card/spend-management platform, Brex's closest competitor.
selfServeDetail: Similar to Brex: must be an approved Ramp business customer before API credentials exist; once a customer, OAuth2 client is self-serve from the admin dashboard.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: Requires an approved Ramp customer account before any API credentials can be created.

name: PitchBook
description: Private-markets/M&A research data platform (companies, deals, investors).
selfServeDetail: No public self-signup of any kind; data access (including API) is sold as part of an enterprise subscription and provisioned by PitchBook's sales/success team.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found.
blocker: Enterprise-only, contact-sales; no public docs or trial access to independently verify the API surface.

name: NotebookLM
description: Google's AI research/notebook assistant; consumer product has no public API of its own.
auth: OAuth2 (via underlying Gemini Enterprise API, not NotebookLM directly)
selfServeDetail: The consumer NotebookLM product has no public developer API at all. Programmatic, notebook-like capability is only available indirectly through Gemini Enterprise, which is an enterprise-sales product.
mcp_notes: No MCP server exists because there is no public API to wrap.
blocker: No public API for the NotebookLM product itself; the only related programmatic path is Gemini Enterprise, a separate contact-sales product, not a NotebookLM integration.
notes: Defeated-app case: this is a real, honest 'no' -- worth flagging clearly for Composio since it's a commonly requested app.
